headline: 2 S&P 500 Stocks with Competitive Advantages and 1 That Underwhelm
summary: The S&P 500 (^GSPC) is home to the biggest and most well-known companies in the market, making it a go-to index for investors seeking stability. But not all large-cap stocks are created equal - some are struggling with slowing growth, declining margins, or increased competition.

headline: An Investor Dared Him to Quit School. Now He’s Building a $1.5 Billion AI Startup.
summary: Matan Grinberg’s Factory, which offers autonomous coding tools, is competing with the likes of Anthropic, OpenAI and Cursor.

headline: Can Platform Wins Accelerate Palo Alto Networks' Long-Term Growth?
summary: PANW's platform strategy is gaining traction with rising customer adoption, large deal wins and strong retention, supporting its long-term revenue growth outlook.

headline: Zscaler Stock Plummets 42% YTD: Should You Hold Tight or Exit?
summary: ZS stock plunges 42% YTD amid the broader industry sell-off, but strong growth, AI bets and improving valuation suggest holding it for now.

headline: Palo Alto Networks founder applies to invest in California bank
summary: It is unclear whether Nir Zuk wants to buy a majority of Liberty Bank, but observers say the lender could become a testing ground for the capabilities of artificial intelligence tools.

headline: Customer Demand for Continuous Packet Capture Drives Upsurge in Endace Partner Program
summary: AUCKLAND, New Zealand & AUSTIN, Texas, April 16, 2026--Packet capture authority, Endace, has announced a raft of new partners and updated integrations as part of its Fusion Partner Program, including Cisco XDR, Splunk SIEM, Splunk SOAR, Vectra AI, Microsoft Sentinel, Google SecOps, Exabeam LogRhythm, Palo Alto Networks XSOAR, Sumo Logic, and Fortinet FortiNDR.

headline: Jim Cramer Says AI Is Not “Gobbling” Stocks Like CrowdStrike, Its “Supporting” Them
summary: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:CRWD) was among the stocks Jim Cramer looked at as he discussed the recent bounce in software stocks. Cramer highlighted the impact of AI on the stock, as he said: Then there are the two stocks of companies that shouldn’t even be grouped with the rest of the software industry at all. […]

headline: Palo Alto Networks Expands AI Security With Koi Deal And AES Launch
summary: Palo Alto Networks (NasdaqGS:PANW) has completed its acquisition of AI security company Koi. The company introduced a new product category, Agentic Endpoint Security (AES), focused on protecting AI powered software on enterprise endpoints. The AES launch is intended to help enterprises identify and remediate risks in their AI software ecosystem. Palo Alto Networks, a large player in network and cloud security, is using the Koi acquisition to expand deeper into protection for AI driven...
